tasks.title: Project Scaffold & Config
tasks.description: Initialize Next.js 14 App Router project with Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, TypeScript strict mode, and the central config object.
tasks.details:
1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest` with TypeScript, Tailwind, App Router, src/ directory.
2. Install shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn@latest init`.
3. Create `src/lib/config.ts` exporting:
   - apiBase = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WP_URL + '/wp-json'
   - productsNs = 'wpadhlwrapi/v1'
   - apiNs = 'api'
   - features: { registration: boolean, guestCheckout: boolean }
4. Create `.env.local.example` with NEXT_PUBLIC_WP_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PK placeholders.
5. Set up src/ directory structure as defined in PRD section 4.1.
6. Add tsconfig.json path aliases: @/* -> ./src/*.

Constraints:
- Zero hardcoded URLs or store names outside config.ts.
- NEXT_PUBLIC_WP_URL is the only variable needed to switch stores.

Files (max 5): src/lib/config.ts, .env.local.example, tsconfig.json, package.json, tailwind.config.ts
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run `npm run build` -- must compile with zero TypeScript errors.
2. Change NEXT_PUBLIC_WP_URL in .env.local -- verify config.apiBase reflects the new value at runtime.
3. Import a shadcn/ui Button in a test page -- verify it renders correctly.

tasks.title: Central API Client
tasks.description: Build lib/api/client.ts -- the single fetch wrapper that attaches JWT, handles 401 auto-refresh with in-flight deduplication, retries on network failure, and normalises errors.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/lib/api/client.ts exporting apiClient(path, options).
2. Token attachment: read access_token from the Authorization cookie (server components) or from authStore (client components).
3. On 401 response:
   a. If a refresh is already in-flight, await the same promise (singleton -- use a module-level `let refreshPromise: Promise | null`).
   b. Otherwise, call POST /api/auth/refresh with the refresh_token cookie.
   c. On refresh success: update access_token cookie, retry original request once.
   d. On refresh failure: call authStore.logout() and router.push('/login').
4. On { success: false }: throw ApiError with { code, message } fields.
5. Network failure: retry once after 500 ms delay.
6. Never expose raw WP/WooCommerce internal fields.

API deps: API Documentation.md section 3 (token types), section 7.2 (refresh endpoint).

Files (max 5): src/lib/api/client.ts, src/lib/errors.ts
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Mock a 401 response followed by a successful refresh -- verify original request is retried with the new token.
2. Mock two simultaneous 401 requests -- verify only ONE refresh call fires.
3. Mock refresh returning 401 -- verify authStore.logout() is called and redirect to /login occurs.
4. Mock { success: false, code: 'out_of_stock', message: '...' } -- verify an ApiError is thrown with matching code.
5. Mock network failure -- verify one retry happens after ~500 ms.

tasks.title: Zustand Auth Store + Next.js Auth API Routes
tasks.description: Create stores/authStore.ts (Zustand) and the Next.js server-side route handlers that set/clear httpOnly JWT cookies.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/stores/authStore.ts with Zustand:
   - State: user: { id, email, display_name } | null, isAuthenticated: boolean, loading: boolean.
   - Actions: login(username, password, guestToken?), register(email, password, first_name, last_name), logout(), refreshToken().
   - login and register call the Next.js API routes (not the WP API directly).

2. Create Next.js Route Handlers:
   - src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts: forwards to POST /wp-json/api/auth/login, sets access_token and refresh_token as httpOnly Secure SameSite=Lax cookies, returns { user }.
   - src/app/api/auth/register/route.ts: forwards to POST /wp-json/api/auth/register, sets cookies, returns { user }.
   - src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts: clears both cookies, returns 200.
   - src/app/api/auth/refresh/route.ts: reads refresh_token cookie, forwards to POST /wp-json/api/auth/refresh, updates access_token cookie.

3. On successful login: call cartStore.clearGuestToken() to remove guest_token from localStorage (cart was merged).

Constraints:
- access_token and refresh_token are NEVER accessible from JavaScript (httpOnly).
- guest_token stays in localStorage; this store does not manage it.

API deps: API Documentation.md sections 7.1, 7.2, Flow E.

Files (max 5): src/stores/authStore.ts, src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts, src/app/api/auth/register/route.ts, src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts, src/app/api/auth/refresh/route.ts
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Call authStore.login() with valid credentials -- verify isAuthenticated = true, user is populated, httpOnly cookie is set (visible in DevTools Cookies, not in JS document.cookie).
2. Call authStore.logout() -- verify cookies are cleared and user = null.
3. After login with a guest token -- verify localStorage cart_token is removed.

tasks.title: Zustand Cart Store + Cart API Layer
tasks.description: Create stores/cartStore.ts and lib/api/cart.ts -- full cart state management with guest token lifecycle and cart-merge on login.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/lib/api/cart.ts with functions:
   - apiAddToCart(productId, variationId, quantity) -- calls POST /wpadhlwrapi/v1/cart/add
   - apiGetCart(token) -- calls GET /wpadhlwrapi/v1/cart
   - apiUpdateCartItem(itemId, quantity) -- calls PUT /wpadhlwrapi/v1/cart/update
   - apiRemoveCartItem(itemId) -- calls DELETE /wpadhlwrapi/v1/cart/remove

2. Create src/stores/cartStore.ts with Zustand:
   - State: items: CartItem[], cartToken: string | null, guestToken: string | null, loading: boolean, error: string | null, cartDrawerOpen: boolean.
   - fetchCart(): fetches cart with current token (guest or user).
   - addItem(productId, variationId, quantity): on first add -- saves returned guest_token to localStorage['cart_token'] and to guestToken state.
   - updateItem(itemId, quantity): calls update API, refreshes cart state.
   - removeItem(itemId): calls remove API, refreshes cart state.
   - clearGuestToken(): removes localStorage['cart_token'], sets guestToken = null.
   - clearCart(): resets items to [] (called after order confirmation).
   - error is always set for API failures -- never swallowed silently.

Edge cases:
- out_of_stock (400) -- set error = 'This item is out of stock'.
- guest_token only appears in the FIRST cart/add response; do not overwrite on subsequent adds.

API deps: API Documentation.md section 6 (all cart endpoints), Flow A steps 3-5.

Files (max 4): src/stores/cartStore.ts, src/lib/api/cart.ts
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Call addItem() with no existing token -- verify localStorage['cart_token'] is set and guestToken populated.
2. Call addItem() a second time -- verify localStorage['cart_token'] is NOT overwritten.
3. Call updateItem() -- verify cart state reflects new quantity.
4. Call removeItem() -- verify item absent from items array.
5. Trigger out_of_stock error -- verify cartStore.error = 'This item is out of stock'.
6. After clearGuestToken() -- verify localStorage['cart_token'] is null.

tasks.title: Login Page
tasks.description: Build /login page with LoginForm component -- credential form, guest cart merge on login, error display.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/login/page.tsx (SSG static page).
2. Create src/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx:
   - Fields: username (email or WP username), password.
   - On submit: reads localStorage['cart_token'] (guest_token); calls authStore.login(username, password, guestToken).
   - On success: redirect to /account (or ?redirect= param if set).
   - Shows inline error for invalid_credentials: 'Invalid username or password.'
   - Submit button: loading spinner while request is in-flight, disabled during loading.
3. Link to /register ('Create an account') and /forgot-password ('Forgot password?').

API deps: API Documentation.md section 7.1.

Files (max 4): src/app/login/page.tsx, src/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 2 (step 1) + scenario 1 (steps 8-9):
1. Submit with wrong credentials -- inline error shown.
2. Submit with valid credentials + existing guest cart -- redirect to /account, cart merged, localStorage cleared.
3. Submit with valid credentials (no guest cart) -- redirect to /account.

tasks.title: Register Page
tasks.description: Build /register page with RegisterForm -- new account creation with immediate login on success.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/register/page.tsx (SSG static page).
2. Create src/components/auth/RegisterForm.tsx:
   - Fields: first_name, last_name, email, password, confirm_password.
   - Client-side validation: email format, password min 8 chars, passwords match.
   - On submit: calls authStore.register(email, password, first_name, last_name).
   - On success (201): redirect to /account; user is immediately authenticated (cookies set by route handler).
   - Error email_exists -- 'This email is already registered.'
   - Error registration_failed -- 'Registration failed. Please try again.'
3. Link to /login ('Already have an account?').

API deps: API Documentation.md Flow E.

Files (max 3): src/app/register/page.tsx, src/components/auth/RegisterForm.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 6:
1. Submit with already-registered email -- 'This email is already registered' error shown.
2. Submit with password < 8 chars -- inline validation error before API call.
3. Submit valid new user -- redirect to /account, user logged in, access_token cookie set (httpOnly -- not in JS).

tasks.title: Forgot Password & Reset Password Pages
tasks.description: Build /forgot-password and /reset-password pages -- two-step password recovery with anti-enumeration security.
tasks.details:
1. Forgot Password:
   - src/app/forgot-password/page.tsx (SSG static).
   - src/components/auth/ForgotPasswordForm.tsx: single email field.
   - Calls POST /wp-json/api/auth/forgot-password via apiClient.
   - ALWAYS shows: 'If an account exists for this email, a reset link has been sent.' -- regardless of API outcome (anti-enumeration).
   - Submit button disabled after first submit to prevent spam.

2. Reset Password:
   - src/app/reset-password/page.tsx (CSR -- reads URL query params).
   - src/components/auth/ResetPasswordForm.tsx: new_password + confirm_password fields.
   - On mount: reads ?key= and ?login= from URL. If either missing -- show 'Invalid reset link.' immediately.
   - On submit: calls POST /wp-json/api/auth/reset-password with { key, login, new_password }.
   - On success: redirect to /login?reset=success.
   - Error invalid_reset_key -- 'This link is invalid or has expired.'

API deps: API Documentation.md Flow F.

Files (max 4): src/app/forgot-password/page.tsx, src/components/auth/ForgotPasswordForm.tsx, src/app/reset-password/page.tsx, src/components/auth/ResetPasswordForm.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 7:
1. Submit forgot-password with non-existent email -- same success message shown (no enumeration).
2. Submit forgot-password with real email -- same message shown; email received.
3. Click reset link -- form shown; fill new password -- redirect to /login with success message.
4. Log in with new password -- succeeds.
5. Use expired reset link -- 'This link is invalid or has expired.' shown.

tasks.title: Product Archive Page
tasks.description: Build /products SSG+ISR page with ProductGrid, ProductCard, pagination, category filter, and search.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/lib/api/products.ts:
   - getProducts({ page, per_page, category, search }) -- GET /wpadhlwrapi/v1/products with next: { revalidate: 60 }.
   - getProduct(slug) -- GET /wpadhlwrapi/v1/products/{slug} with ISR.
   - getAllProductSlugs() -- fetches all pages to build generateStaticParams.

2. Create src/app/(shop)/products/page.tsx:
   - Server Component (SSG+ISR revalidate: 60).
   - Reads searchParams: page, category, search.
   - Clamps per_page to 12 (never > 100).
   - Renders ProductGrid and pagination controls.
   - Empty state: 'No products found.'

3. Create src/components/product/ProductCard.tsx:
   - Displays: next/image thumbnail, name, price (sale vs regular with strikethrough), On Sale badge.
   - Links to /products/{slug}.

4. Create src/components/product/ProductGrid.tsx:
   - Responsive grid layout; maps products array to ProductCard components.

API deps: API Documentation.md section 5.1.

Files (max 5): src/app/(shop)/products/page.tsx, src/lib/api/products.ts, src/components/product/ProductCard.tsx, src/components/product/ProductGrid.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 3 (steps 1-2):
1. Visit /products -- verify product grid renders (Network tab: no auth headers).
2. Add ?category=t-shirts -- verify filtered results.
3. Add ?search=tee -- verify search results.
4. Paginate via ?page=2 -- verify new products load.
5. Visit with no matching filter -- verify 'No products found.' message.
6. `npm run build` -- verify ISR pages are statically generated.

tasks.title: Product Detail Page
tasks.description: Build /products/[slug] SSG+ISR page with image gallery, VariationSelector, and Add to Cart with variation enforcement.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/(shop)/products/[slug]/page.tsx:
   - Server Component with generateStaticParams (uses getAllProductSlugs()).
   - ISR: export const revalidate = 60.
   - Calls getProduct(slug) -- uses fat payload (no extra variation requests).
   - On product_not_found (null) -- notFound().
   - Renders: image gallery (next/image), name, price (sale vs regular), short_description, description, VariationSelector, Add to Cart button.

2. Create src/components/product/VariationSelector.tsx:
   - Client Component (needs state for selected attributes).
   - For variable products: renders selector for each attribute group in variations[].attributes.
   - Add to Cart DISABLED until all attribute groups have a selection.
   - Selected variation with stock_status === 'outofstock' -- button shows 'Out of Stock', disabled.
   - For simple products (empty variations[]): renders Add to Cart directly.
   - On Add to Cart click: calls cartStore.addItem(product.id, selectedVariation.id, 1).
   - Shows loading state during cart API call.

Edge cases:
- Product image url missing -- render a placeholder image.
- Simple product -- no variation UI rendered.

API deps: API Documentation.md sections 5.2 and 6.1.

Files (max 4): src/app/(shop)/products/[slug]/page.tsx, src/components/product/VariationSelector.tsx, src/lib/api/products.ts (already created in task 8)
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenarios 1 (steps 3-5), 3 (steps 2-3), 5 (steps 1-3):
1. Navigate to a variable product -- verify variation selectors render.
2. Click Add to Cart WITHOUT selecting all attributes -- verify button stays disabled.
3. Select all attributes -- verify button activates.
4. Select an out-of-stock variation -- verify button shows 'Out of Stock', disabled.
5. Navigate to /products/nonexistent-slug -- verify 404 page.
6. Navigate to a simple product -- verify no variation selectors, Add to Cart immediately active.

tasks.title: Cart Page + Cart Drawer
tasks.description: Build /cart CSR page with CartItem, CartSummary, and a slide-out CartDrawer accessible from the Header.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/cart/page.tsx:
   - Client Component ('use client'). Calls cartStore.fetchCart() on mount.
   - Renders list of CartItem components.
   - Empty state: 'Your cart is empty.' with 'Continue Shopping' link -> /products.
   - 'Proceed to Checkout' button -> /checkout.

2. Create src/components/cart/CartItem.tsx:
   - Displays: product name, variation attributes (from item meta), quantity stepper (+/- buttons), remove button.
   - Quantity stepper: calls cartStore.updateItem(item.id, newQty). Min quantity = 1.
   - Remove button: calls cartStore.removeItem(item.id).
   - NOTE (OQ-1): Cart API does not return item prices. Display quantity only; subtotal omitted pending resolution of OQ-1.

3. Create src/components/cart/CartSummary.tsx:
   - Displays item count. Subtotal field shows 'Calculated at checkout' (OQ-1 pending).
   - 'Go to Checkout' button.

4. Create src/components/cart/CartDrawer.tsx:
   - shadcn/ui Sheet (slide-over panel) opened via cartStore.cartDrawerOpen boolean.
   - Renders CartItem list in compact form.
   - 'View Cart' -> /cart; 'Checkout' -> /checkout.

5. Update src/components/layout/Header.tsx:
   - Cart icon button shows badge with cartStore.items.length.
   - Click opens CartDrawer.

API deps: API Documentation.md sections 6.2, 6.3, 6.4.

Files (max 5): src/app/cart/page.tsx, src/components/cart/CartItem.tsx, src/components/cart/CartSummary.tsx, src/components/cart/CartDrawer.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 1 (steps 6-7) and scenario 2 (step 2):
1. Add item to cart -- CartDrawer opens, badge shows count = 1.
2. Navigate to /cart -- item renders with correct name and quantity.
3. Change quantity via stepper -- update reflected without page reload.
4. Remove item -- item disappears from list.
5. Remove all items -- empty state message shown.

tasks.title: Checkout Page - Address Form & Gateway Selector
tasks.description: Build protected /checkout CSR page with billing/shipping address form pre-filled from user profile and payment gateway selector.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/checkout/page.tsx:
   - Client Component ('use client'). Protected: if not authenticated -- redirect to /login?redirect=/checkout.
   - If cartStore.items.length === 0 on mount -- redirect to /cart with message 'Your cart is empty.'
   - On mount: fetch GET /api/user to pre-fill billing form fields.

2. Create src/components/checkout/CheckoutForm.tsx:
   - Fields (matching API Documentation.md section 7.3 address object): first_name, last_name, company, address_1, address_2, city, state, postcode, country, email, phone.
   - Shipping address: checkbox 'Same as billing' (default checked). If unchecked -- shows separate shipping fields (no email/phone).
   - Gateway selector: radio buttons -- 'Credit Card (Stripe)' | 'Bank Transfer (BACS)'.
   - Renders StripeForm (task 12) or BacsInfo (task 13) based on selection.
   - Submit button: shows loading spinner, disabled during API call.
   - All error codes from API Documentation.md section 7.3 mapped to visible messages.

3. Create src/lib/api/checkout.ts:
   - placeOrder({ gateway, payment_data?, billing, shipping? }) -- POST /wp-json/api/checkout with access_token from cookie.
   - Returns { order_id, status, transaction_id }.

API deps: API Documentation.md sections 7.3 and 9.1 (user pre-fill).

Files (max 5): src/app/checkout/page.tsx, src/components/checkout/CheckoutForm.tsx, src/lib/api/checkout.ts
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 1 (step 10) and scenario 2 (steps 3-4):
1. Navigate to /checkout without login -- redirected to /login.
2. Log in, navigate to /checkout -- form pre-filled with saved billing address.
3. Verify 'Same as billing' checkbox pre-checked; uncheck -- shipping fields appear.
4. Select BACS -- verify BacsInfo renders (no card field).
5. Select Stripe -- verify StripeForm (CardElement) renders.

tasks.title: Checkout - Stripe Integration
tasks.description: Build StripeForm with @stripe/react-stripe-js CardElement, client-side PaymentMethod creation, and POST /api/checkout stripe flow.
tasks.details:
1. Install: npm install @stripe/stripe-js @stripe/react-stripe-js.
2. Create src/components/checkout/StripeForm.tsx:
   - Wrapped in Elements stripe={stripePromise} (lazy-loaded via dynamic() to avoid SSR issues).
   - stripePromise = loadStripe(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PK!).
   - Renders shadcn/ui-styled CardElement container.
   - On parent form submit:
     a. stripe.createPaymentMethod({ type: 'card', card: cardElement }) -- get paymentMethod.id.
     b. If Stripe returns an error -- display error.message inline; STOP (do not call backend).
     c. Calls CheckoutForm.handlePlaceOrder({ gateway: 'stripe', payment_data: { payment_method_id } }).
3. Add NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PK to .env.local.example.
4. Import StripeForm in CheckoutForm via dynamic(() => import('./StripeForm'), { ssr: false }) (code-split, no SSR).

Edge cases:
- payment_failed (402 from backend) -- 'Payment was declined. Please check your card details.'
- Card validation error before submit -- show Stripe error message in a visible red label.

API deps: API Documentation.md section 7.3 (stripe gateway), Flow A steps 8-9.

Files (max 4): src/components/checkout/StripeForm.tsx, src/components/checkout/CheckoutForm.tsx (add dynamic import), .env.local.example
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 1 (steps 11-12):
1. Select Stripe, enter test card 4242 4242 4242 4242 exp 12/34 CVC 123.
2. Submit -- verify redirect to /order-confirmation/{id} with status 'completed'.
3. Enter declined card 4000 0000 0000 0002 -- verify 'Payment was declined.' error shown; no redirect.
4. Submit with empty card field -- verify Stripe inline validation error before API call.

tasks.title: Checkout - BACS Integration
tasks.description: Build BacsInfo component for Bank Transfer checkout -- displays bank details notice and submits BACS order.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/components/checkout/BacsInfo.tsx:
   - Renders a notice box: 'You will receive bank transfer instructions by email after placing your order.'
   - No additional form fields required for BACS.
2. In CheckoutForm.tsx when gateway = 'bacs':
   - Submit calls placeOrder({ gateway: 'bacs', billing, shipping }) (no payment_data).
   - On success { order_id, status: 'pending' } -- redirect to /order-confirmation/{order_id}.

API deps: API Documentation.md section 7.3 (bacs gateway), Flow B.

Files (max 2): src/components/checkout/BacsInfo.tsx, src/components/checkout/CheckoutForm.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 2 (steps 3-4):
1. Select BACS, fill billing form, submit.
2. Verify redirect to /order-confirmation/{id} with status 'pending'.
3. Verify no card element rendered when BACS is selected.

tasks.title: Order Confirmation Page
tasks.description: Build /order-confirmation/[id] CSR page -- shows order status, transaction ID (Stripe) or bank transfer instructions (BACS).
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/order-confirmation/[id]/page.tsx:
   - Client Component ('use client').
   - Reads order data from router state or sessionStorage (passed by checkout on redirect).
   - Displays:
     - 'Order #42 confirmed!' heading.
     - Status badge (Completed / Pending Payment).
     - If transaction_id present (Stripe): 'Payment ID: pi_xxx'.
     - If status === 'pending' (BACS): bank transfer instructions block with order number reference.
   - 'Continue Shopping' button -- /products.
   - 'View My Orders' button -- /account.
2. After rendering: call cartStore.clearCart() to reset cart state.

Files (max 2): src/app/order-confirmation/[id]/page.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
1. After Stripe checkout success -- order confirmation shows order_id and 'Completed' status.
2. After BACS checkout success -- order confirmation shows 'Pending Payment' and bank transfer instructions.
3. Navigate to /account after confirmation -- order appears in order list.

tasks.title: Account Page
tasks.description: Build protected /account CSR page -- user profile display, saved addresses, paginated order history, logout.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/account/page.tsx:
   - Client Component ('use client'). Protected: if not authenticated -- redirect to /login.
   - On mount: call getUserProfile() and getOrders(1, 10) in parallel.

2. Create src/lib/api/user.ts:
   - getUserProfile() -- GET /wp-json/api/user with access_token cookie.

3. Create src/lib/api/orders.ts:
   - getOrders(page, perPage) -- GET /wp-json/api/orders?page=&per_page= with access_token cookie.

4. Profile section (read-only in v1): display_name, email, billing address block, shipping address block.

5. Create src/components/account/OrdersList.tsx:
   - Paginated list (previous/next buttons).
   - Each row: Order #, date (formatted), status badge (color-coded), total with currency.
   - Empty state: 'You have no orders yet.'

6. Logout button: calls authStore.logout(), redirects to /.

Edge cases:
- user_not_found (404 from /api/user) -- call authStore.logout(), redirect to /login.
- invalid_token (401) -- auto-refresh via apiClient; if refresh fails -- redirect to /login.

API deps: API Documentation.md sections 9.1 (orders), 10.1 (user profile), Flow D.

Files (max 5): src/app/account/page.tsx, src/lib/api/user.ts, src/lib/api/orders.ts, src/components/account/OrdersList.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
Manual test scenario 2 (step 5) and scenario 4 (token expiry):
1. Log in, navigate to /account -- profile data shown, order list loads.
2. Verify pagination: navigate to page 2 if orders > 10.
3. Click Logout -- redirected to /, cookies cleared.
4. Navigate to /account without login -- redirected to /login.
5. With expired access token + valid refresh token -- page still loads (auto-refresh).
6. With both tokens expired -- redirected to /login with session expired message.

tasks.title: Home Page
tasks.description: Build / SSG+ISR home page with a config-driven hero section and featured products grid.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/(shop)/page.tsx:
   - Server Component with export const revalidate = 60.
   - Calls getProducts({ per_page: 8 }) for featured products.
   - Renders hero section and ProductGrid.

2. Hero section driven by lib/config.ts hero object:
   - config.hero = { title, subtitle, ctaText, ctaLink } -- all values from env vars or config.
   - No hardcoded marketing copy.

3. 'Shop All' CTA button -> /products.

Constraints:
- No direct WooCommerce dependency.
- Changing NEXT_PUBLIC_WP_URL changes the product source; no other code changes needed.

Files (max 3): src/app/(shop)/page.tsx, src/lib/config.ts (add hero config)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. `npm run build` -- verify home page is statically generated.
2. Visit / -- hero section renders with config values, 8 products shown.
3. Click a product card -- navigates to /products/[slug].
4. Click 'Shop All' -- navigates to /products.

tasks.title: Layout - Header & Footer
tasks.description: Build site-wide Header and Footer -- navigation, auth-aware links, cart icon with badge, mobile responsive.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/components/layout/Header.tsx:
   - Client Component (reads Zustand state).
   - Logo/site name from config.ts.
   - Navigation links: Home (/), Products (/products).
   - Auth-aware right section:
     - Unauthenticated: 'Login' -> /login, 'Register' -> /register.
     - Authenticated: 'Account' -> /account, 'Logout' button.
   - Cart icon: shows cartStore.items.length badge (0 hidden).
   - Cart icon click: sets cartStore.cartDrawerOpen = true.
   - Mobile: hamburger menu (shadcn/ui Sheet).

2. Create src/components/layout/Footer.tsx:
   - Static links: Privacy Policy, Terms, Contact.
   - Copyright text from config.ts.

3. Add Header + Footer to src/app/layout.tsx.

Constraints:
- No WooCommerce dependency.
- Must render correctly when cart is empty and user is not logged in.

Files (max 4): src/components/layout/Header.tsx, src/components/layout/Footer.tsx, src/app/layout.tsx
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Visit any page unauthenticated -- Header shows Login + Register links; cart badge hidden.
2. Log in -- Header shows Account + Logout; Login/Register hidden.
3. Add item to cart -- cart badge shows count.
4. Click cart icon -- CartDrawer opens.
5. Resize to mobile (375px) -- hamburger menu appears, nav collapses.

tasks.title: Error Handling & 404 Pages
tasks.description: Add global error boundary, 404 not-found page, and API error toast notifications.
tasks.details:
1. Create src/app/error.tsx (Next.js global error boundary):
   - Client Component. Shows 'Something went wrong. Please try again.' with a Reset button.
   - Logs error to console.error in dev.

2. Create src/app/not-found.tsx:
   - Shows '404 - Page not found' with link back to /products.

3. Add a toast notification system:
   - Use sonner (or shadcn/ui Toaster) for non-blocking API error messages.
   - apiClient.ts calls toast.error(error.message) on API errors where appropriate.
   - Cart errors (out_of_stock) surface via cartStore.error AND a toast.

4. Product 404: notFound() is already called in task 9. Verify not-found.tsx is reached.

Constraints: Fail loudly -- every error visible to user; no silent catch.

Files (max 4): src/app/error.tsx, src/app/not-found.tsx, src/components/ui/toaster.tsx (shadcn), src/app/layout.tsx (add Toaster)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Navigate to /nonexistent-page -- 404 not-found page shown.
2. Navigate to /products/nonexistent-slug -- 404 shown.
3. Trigger an API error (e.g., network disconnect) -- error toast appears in UI.
4. Out-of-stock add to cart -- toast shows 'This item is out of stock.'

tasks.title: Manual QA & Integration Testing
tasks.description: Execute all 7 manual test scenarios from PRD section 8 against a live staging backend. Document pass/fail.
tasks.details:
Execute all 7 manual test scenarios from PRD section 8 (source: .taskmaster/docs/headless_ecommerce_frontend_prd_bak.txt):

1. Scenario 1 - Guest Purchase (Stripe): Full flow from product browse -> add to cart -> login with cart merge -> Stripe checkout -> order confirmation.
2. Scenario 2 - Registered User Purchase (BACS): Login -> browse -> add simple product -> BACS checkout -> order in account.
3. Scenario 3 - Browse Without Buying: Product listing + PDP load without auth; 404 on bad slug.
4. Scenario 4 - Token Expiry / Auto-Refresh: Simulate expired access token; verify silent refresh; simulate expired refresh token; verify redirect to /login.
5. Scenario 5 - Out-of-Stock Error: Mark product out of stock; verify Add to Cart disabled; verify error on cart add; verify checkout error.
6. Scenario 6 - New User Registration: Duplicate email error; password validation; successful registration -> auto-login.
7. Scenario 7 - Forgot/Reset Password: Anti-enumeration message; full reset flow; expired link error.

Test environments: Chrome desktop + Safari mobile (375px viewport).
Record results as PASS / FAIL / BLOCKED in a QA report.
tasks.testStrategy: Each scenario IS the test. All 7 scenarios must show PASS before v1 is considered shippable. Document any FAIL or BLOCKED with screenshots and reproduction steps.
